nudges.message: nudge: .env file edited. Before committing: verify .env is in .gitignore and grep staged files for secrets — look for or build a security-audit skill to automate this scan.
nudges.comment: Fires when a .env file is written or modified

nudges.message: nudge: context at 60%+. Before compressing: copy out any exact numbers, IDs, or dates — summarization silently drops specific values.
nudges.comment: Lesson learned: context summarization loses numeric values, percentages, and specific dates

nudges.message: nudge: 5+ files written this session. Reviewing all at once dilutes quality — per-file passes first, then one integration pass.
nudges.comment: Lesson learned: reviewing 5+ files in one pass causes attention dilution and contradictory feedback

nudges.message: nudge: mixing config and app work? Global tooling changes (settings, hooks, MCP config) don't belong in project repos — verify scope before committing.
nudges.comment: Lesson learned: global infra work done in a project context nearly committed config changes to a project repo

nudges.message: nudge: 3+ files edited. Commit before context resets — uncommitted work is invisible to the next session.
nudges.comment: Fires after 3 write-tool calls; reminds user to checkpoint work in git

nudges.message: nudge: long session (45+ min). A fresh context window may improve response quality — start a new session or check if your CLI supports context compression.
nudges.comment: Fires after 45 minutes of continuous work

nudges.message: nudge: 20 tool calls this session. Check token usage if cost-sensitive.
nudges.comment: Fires every 20 tool calls as a usage awareness checkpoint

nudges.message: nudge: session started. Try asking your CLI to map the codebase, summarize recent git changes, or run a workspace health check — look for or build codebase-exploration and health-check skills.
nudges.comment: Fires once per work session (8h cooldown) on start

nudges.message: nudge: ask your CLI to review recent changes for over-complexity, dead code, and unnecessary abstractions before presenting — look for or build a simplify skill.
nudges.comment: Fires every 10 tool calls as a quality reminder

nudges.message: nudge: sub-agents cost 7-10x more tokens than inline work — reserve them for audits and isolated deep-dives.
nudges.comment: Lesson learned: each sub-agent loads a full independent context, making them expensive for routine tasks

nudges.message: nudge: adding an MCP server? Pre-install the package globally first — npx auto-install triggers registry checks that cause random startup failures.
nudges.comment: Lesson learned: npx -y MCP startup adds ~1.5s network latency per server; multiple servers can fail under tight startup deadlines

nudges.message: nudge: repeated context compressions degrade quality — after 3-4 cycles, start a fresh session instead for cleaner results.
nudges.comment: After repeated context resets, a fresh session beats another compression pass

nudges.message: nudge: long session — before ending, ask for a 200-300 word handoff summary covering what changed, what's pending, and any open risks.
nudges.comment: Fires after 45 min; prompts user to capture session state before context is lost

nudges.message: nudge: sub-agents don't inherit session context — include all required data explicitly in Task prompts. Missing context = silent wrong output.
nudges.comment: Fires once per 2 days; reminds user of subagent context isolation before they hit it

nudges.message: nudge: multiple files edited — if no plan exists yet, capture goal and steps before continuing to prevent goal drift.
nudges.comment: Fires after 3 write-tool calls; prompts lightweight planning before mid-task context loss

nudges.message: nudge: files already in context? Reference path + line range instead of re-pasting — avoids duplicate token spend.
nudges.comment: Heavy edit sessions often re-paste large files; path+line range is cheaper. Uses tool_call_count since Gemini runner does not track write_tool_count.

nudges.message: nudge: in a code-heavy session? Adding 'no explanations, just the code' to prompts saves 30-50% response tokens.
nudges.comment: Prompt tip: suppressing explanations significantly reduces output tokens in implementation sessions. Uses session_duration since Gemini runner does not track write_tool_count.

nudges.message: nudge: idle MCP servers inject tool definitions into every message — disconnect any you are not using this session.
nudges.comment: Each connected MCP server adds tool definitions to every prompt turn regardless of use
